metaDescription: Driver registration, tech inspection, division rules, and spectator info for Chemung Speedrome. Everything you need for race night.

drivers.headline: Driver Information
drivers.intro: Everything you need to show up ready to race. If your question isn't answered here, reach out before race day — not after you've already loaded the trailer.

sections.title: Registration & Check-In
items: Driver registration opens at 3:00 PM on race nights. Check-in closes 30 minutes before scheduled hot laps.
First-time competitors must register in person at the pit office and provide a valid driver's license.
Annual membership forms are available at the pit office or by contacting us ahead of your first race.
All cars must be registered and numbered before entering the pit area. Duplicate numbers will be resolved by the pit steward on a first-come basis.
Transponders are required for all divisions. Loaner units are available at the pit office for a $20 deposit.

sections.title: Tech Inspection
items: Tech lane opens at 3:00 PM and closes 15 minutes before the start of hot laps.
All cars must pass tech before they are permitted on the racing surface, no exceptions.
Post-race tech is conducted on top finishers in every feature. Disqualification for tech failure is final.
Bring your rule book. Tech officials reference the current season rulebook — published on this site — for every inspection.
Protest procedures and fees are outlined in the rulebook. All protests must be filed before the completion of the event.

sections.title: Divisions & Classes
items: Chemung Speedrome currently sanctions five divisions: Sportsman Modified, Limited Late Model, Street Stock, Pure Stock, and 4-Cylinder.
Full division rules and eligibility requirements are available in the 2026 rulebook — contact us to request a copy.
Engine, tire, and body rules are division-specific. Review your division's section carefully before building or modifying a car.
Questions about class eligibility should be directed to the tech director before race day, not at the tech lane.

sections.title: Track Rules
items: Aggressive or retaliatory driving will result in immediate black-flag disqualification and may result in suspension.
All drivers are expected to conduct themselves professionally in the pit area, on pit road, and on the racing surface.
Alcohol is not permitted in the pit area at any time. Violation results in removal from the facility.
Pit speed limit is 10 MPH. The pace car sets the pit entrance and exit pace during caution periods.
The flagman and race director have final authority over all on-track decisions.

sections.title: Payouts
items: Feature payouts are posted in the pit office at the start of each race night.
Payout structure is based on car count — a minimum car count per division is required for full payout.
All payouts are issued in cash at the pit office following the completion of tech on that night's features.
Points fund details and end-of-season championship payouts will be published ahead of the 2026 season opener.

spectators.headline: Spectator Information
spectators.intro: First time at Chemung Speedrome or coming back after a while — here's what you need to know to have a good night.

sections.title: Getting Here
items: Chemung Speedrome is located at 1200 Speedway Blvd, Millbrook, NY 12545.
From the Taconic State Parkway, take the Millbrook exit and head east on Route 44 for approximately 3 miles, then turn south on Speedway Blvd.
GPS directions to '1200 Speedway Blvd, Millbrook, NY' are reliable. Do not use directions that route through Barker Road — that entrance is for competitors only.
Questions about directions? Call us at (845) 555-0182 on race day.

sections.title: Parking
items: General parking is free and located in the main lot off Speedway Blvd.
Overflow parking is available in the field lot on the north side of the property — attendants will direct you.
RV and trailer parking is available in the designated area past the main lot. No hookups are provided.
Accessible parking spaces are located closest to the main grandstand entrance — display a valid placard to use these spaces.

sections.title: Admission
items: General admission: $12 per person.
Children under 12: Free.
Pit passes: $30 — includes access to the pit area throughout the event.
Season passes are not available for the 2026 season but may be offered in future seasons.
Admission is paid at the main gate. Cash is preferred; card payment is available.

sections.title: What to Bring
items: Ear protection is strongly recommended, especially for children — racing is loud.
Dress for the weather. The grandstands are open-air and evenings in Millbrook can get cool, even in summer.
Seat cushions and folding chairs are welcome in the general admission areas.
Outside food and non-alcoholic beverages in sealed containers are permitted in the grandstands.
Alcohol purchased at the concession stand may be consumed in designated areas only.

sections.title: Food & Concessions
items: The Chemung Speedrome concession stand is open from gate time through the completion of the final feature.
We serve hot dogs, burgers, nachos, popcorn, soft drinks, water, and beer.
A food truck may be on site for select events — follow our social media for announcements.
Concession stand accepts cash and card.
